description: Lightweight qualitative output suite for the issue-normalization skill. These cases are meant for human review after running the skill on representative issue intake, review follow-up, handoff conversion, and split recommendation tasks.

assertion_definitions.source_fidelity: The output preserves where the issue came from and does not erase the original signal.
assertion_definitions.problem_legible: The core problem is understandable without replaying the whole source conversation.
assertion_definitions.scope_bounded: The output keeps one primary issue boundary or explicitly recommends a split.
assertion_definitions.acceptance_criteria_usable: The acceptance criteria are observable and issue-level rather than fake implementation detail.
assertion_definitions.uncertainty_honest: Missing information, weak inferences, or evidence gaps are stated plainly.
assertion_definitions.next_step_clear: The output names the next workflow, owner, or routing decision clearly.

cases.name: Messy bug intake with partial evidence
cases.repo_archetype: Product or service repo with ad-hoc bug reports
cases.prompt: Turn this mixed bug complaint into a normalized issue draft without pretending we know more than we do.
success_criteria: Preserves the source and current evidence instead of over-polishing it away.
Names the missing repro or environment details explicitly.
Produces acceptance criteria that can guide later shaping or debugging.

cases.name: Review finding becomes tracked follow-up work
cases.prompt: Normalize this review finding into a follow-up issue that can be tracked after the current PR lands.
success_criteria: Turns the finding into one concrete issue rather than a vague reminder.
Ends with a sensible next workflow or owner.

cases.name: Handoff notes become a durable issue
cases.repo_archetype: Agentic or ops-heavy repo with run handoffs
cases.prompt: Take this handoff summary and write the canonical issue draft we should continue next.
success_criteria: Turns run or handoff residue into a durable issue shape.
Keeps blockers, evidence, and next-step context visible.
Does not drift into full implementation planning.

cases.name: One raw note should split into multiple issues
cases.repo_archetype: Repo with mixed product and engineering backlog notes
cases.prompt: Normalize these mixed notes into issue candidates and say clearly if they should split.
success_criteria: Detects when one source contains more than one issue boundary.
Explains the split by acceptance boundary, owner, or system impact.
Leaves each resulting issue candidate more actionable than the original bundle.
